<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\frontend\src\slices\notificationsSlice.js`

```text
     1→import { createSlice } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→const initialState = {
     4→  notifications: [],
     5→  unreadCount: 0,
     6→}
     7→
     8→const notificationsSlice = createSlice({
     9→  name: 'notifications',
    10→  initialState,
    11→  reducers: {
    12→    addNotification: (state, action) => {
    13→      state.notifications.unshift(action.payload)
    14→      if (!action.payload.isRead) {
    15→        state.unreadCount += 1
    16→      }
    17→    },
    18→    markAsRead: (state, action) => {
    19→      const notification = state.notifications.find((n) => n._id === action.payload)
    20→      if (notification && !notification.isRead) {
    21→        notification.isRead = true
    22→        state.unreadCount = Math.max(0, state.unreadCount - 1)
    23→      }
    24→    },
    25→    markAllAsRead: (state) => {
    26→      state.notifications = state.notifications.map((n) => ({ ...n, isRead: true }))
    27→      state.unreadCount = 0
    28→    },
    29→    setNotifications: (state, action) => {
    30→      state.notifications = action.payload.notifications || action.payload || []
    31→      state.unreadCount = action.payload.unreadCount || 0
    32→    },
    33→    clearNotifications: (state) => {
    34→      state.notifications = []
    35→      state.unreadCount = 0
    36→    },
    37→  },
    38→})
    39→
    40→export const {
    41→  addNotification,
    42→  markAsRead,
    43→  markAllAsRead,
    44→  setNotifications,
    45→  clearNotifications,
    46→} = notificationsSlice.actions
    47→
    48→export const selectUnreadCount = (state) => state.notifications.unreadCount
    49→export default notificationsSlice.reducer
    50→
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\frontend\src\slices\bookingsSlice.js`

```text
     1→import { createSlice } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→const initialState = {
     4→  bookings: [],
     5→  currentBooking: null,
     6→}
     7→
     8→const bookingsSlice = createSlice({
     9→  name: 'bookings',
    10→  initialState,
    11→  reducers: {
    12→    setBookings: (state, action) => {
    13→      state.bookings = action.payload
    14→    },
    15→    addBooking: (state, action) => {
    16→      state.bookings.unshift(action.payload)
    17→    },
    18→    updateBooking: (state, action) => {
    19→      const idx = state.bookings.findIndex((b) => b._id === action.payload._id)
    20→      if (idx >= 0) state.bookings[idx] = action.payload
    21→      if (state.currentBooking?._id === action.payload._id) {
    22→        state.currentBooking = action.payload
    23→      }
    24→    },
    25→    setCurrentBooking: (state, action) => {
    26→      state.currentBooking = action.payload
    27→    },
    28→    clearCurrentBooking: (state) => {
    29→      state.currentBooking = null
    30→    },
    31→  },
    32→})
    33→
    34→export const { setBookings, addBooking, updateBooking, setCurrentBooking, clearCurrentBooking } =
    35→  bookingsSlice.actions
    36→export default bookingsSlice.reducer
    37→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→const User = require("../models/User.model");
     2→const Food = require("../models/Food.model");
     3→const Booking = require("../models/Booking.model");
     4→const { USER_ROLES } = require("../utils/constants");
     5→const asyncHandler = require("../utils/asyncHandler");
     6→const ApiResponse = require("../utils/ApiResponse");
     7→
     8→const getStats = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
     9→  const now = new Date();
    10→  const sevenDaysAgo = new Date(now.getTime() - 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
    11→
    12→  const totalRevenue = await Booking.aggregate([
    13→    { $match: { status: { $nin: ["cancelled"] }, paymentStatus: "paid" } },
    14→    { $group: { _id: null, total: { $sum: "$total" } } },
    15→  ]);
    16→
    17→  const totalOrders = await Booking.countDocuments({ status: { $ne: "cancelled" } });
    18→  const totalUsers = await User.countDocuments({ role: USER_ROLES.CUSTOMER });
    19→  const totalFoods = await Food.countDocuments();
    20→
    21→  const topFoods = await Booking.aggregate([
    22→    { $unwind: "$items" },
    23→    { $group: { _id: "$items.food", name: { $first: "$items.name" }, totalSold: { $sum: "$items.quantity" } } },
    24→    { $sort: { totalSold: -1 } },
    25→    { $limit: 10 },
    26→    { $lookup: { from: "foods", localField: "_id", foreignField: "_id", as: "food" } },
    27→    { $unwind: "$food" },
    28→    { $project: { name: 1, totalSold: 1, image: "$food.images" } },
    29→  ]);
    30→
    31→  const ordersByStatus = await Booking.aggregate([
    32→    { $group: { _id: "$status", count: { $sum: 1 } } },
    33→  ]);
    34→
    35→  const revenueByDay = await Booking.aggregate([
    36→    { $match: { createdAt: { $gte: sevenDaysAgo }, status: { $nin: ["cancelled"] }, paymentStatus: "paid" } },
    37→    {
    38→      $group: {
    39→        _id: { $dateToString: { format: "%Y-%m-%d", date: "$createdAt" } },
    40→        revenue: { $sum: "$total" },
    41→        orders: { $sum: 1 },
    42→      },
    43→    },
    44→    { $sort: { _id: 1 } },
    45→  ]);
    46→
    47→  return res.status(200).json(
    48→    new ApiResponse(200, {
    49→      totalRevenue: totalRevenue[0]?.total || 0,
    50→      totalOrders,
    51→      totalUsers,
    52→      totalFoods,
    53→      topFoods,
    54→      ordersByStatus,
    55→      revenueByDay,
    56→    })
    57→  );
    58→});
    59→
    60→const getAllUsers = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    61→  const { page = 1, limit = 20, role } = req.query;
    62→  const query = {};
    63→  if (role) query.role = role;
    64→
    65→  const skip = (Number(page) - 1) * Number(limit);
    66→  const total = await User.countDocuments(query);
    67→  const users = await User.find(query).sort("-createdAt").skip(skip).limit(Number(limit));
    68→
    69→  return res.status(200).json(
    70→    new ApiResponse(200, {
    71→      users,
    72→      pagination: { total, page: Number(page), pages: Math.ceil(total / Number(limit)) },
    73→    })
    74→  );
    75→});
    76→
    77→const getShippers = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    78→  const shippers = await User.find({ role: USER_ROLES.SHIPPER }).select("name phone email avatar");
    79→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, shippers));
    80→});
    81→
    82→const getAllOrders = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    83→  const { page = 1, limit = 20, status } = req.query;
    84→  const query = {};
    85→  if (status) query.status = status;
    86→
    87→  const skip = (Number(page) - 1) * Number(limit);
    88→  const total = await Booking.countDocuments(query);
    89→  const orders = await Booking.find(query)
    90→    .sort("-createdAt")
    91→    .skip(skip)
    92→    .limit(Number(limit))
    93→    .populate("user", "name email phone")
    94→    .populate("items.food", "name images price")
    95→    .populate("shipper", "name phone");
    96→
    97→  return res.status(200).json(
    98→    new ApiResponse(200, {
    99→      orders,
   100→      pagination: { total, page: Number(page), pages: Math.ceil(total / Number(limit)) },
   101→    })
   102→  );
   103→});
   104→
   105→module.exports = {
   106→  getStats,
   107→  getAllUsers,
   108→  getShippers,
   109→  getAllOrders,
   110→};
   111→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\backend\src\middlewares\auth.middleware.js`

```text
     1→const jwt = require("jsonwebtoken");
     2→const asyncHandler = require("../utils/asyncHandler");
     3→const User = require("../models/User.model");
     4→
     5→const verifyToken = asyncHandler(async (req, res, next) => {
     6→  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;
     7→  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
     8→    return res.status(401).json({ message: "No token provided" });
     9→  }
    10→
    11→  const token = authHeader.split(" ")[1];
    12→
    13→  try {
    14→    const decoded = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
    15→    const user = await User.findById(decoded.id);
    16→    if (!user) {
    17→      return res.status(401).json({ message: "User not found" });
    18→    }
    19→    req.user = user;
    20→    next();
    21→  } catch (error) {
    22→    return res.status(401).json({ message: "Invalid or expired token" });
    23→  }
    24→});
    25→
    26→const optionalAuth = asyncHandler(async (req, res, next) => {
    27→  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;
    28→  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
    29→    req.user = null;
    30→    return next();
    31→  }
    32→
    33→  const token = authHeader.split(" ")[1];
    34→
    35→  try {
    36→    const decoded = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
    37→    const user = await User.findById(decoded.id);
    38→    req.user = user;
    39→  } catch {
    40→    req.user = null;
    41→  }
    42→  next();
    43→});
    44→
    45→const restrictTo = (...roles) => {
    46→  return (req, res, next) => {
    47→    if (!req.user) {
    48→      return res.status(401).json({ message: "Authentication required" });
    49→    }
    50→    if (!roles.includes(req.user.role)) {
    51→      return res.status(403).json({ message: "Access denied" });
    52→    }
    53→    next();
    54→  };
    55→};
    56→
    57→module.exports = { verifyToken, optionalAuth, restrictTo };
    58→
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I notice that `AdminRoute.jsx` checks for `user?.role !== 'admin' && user?.role !== 'manager'`, but the backend only uses `'admin'`. The backend uses `restrictTo("admin")` for admin endpoints. The frontend `AdminRoute` also allows `'manager'` role, which doesn't exist in the backend. This is fine - the frontend will just redirect non-admin users. The backend will reject managers because they won't pass the `restrictTo("admin")` check.

Actually wait, if a user with role `'manager'` tries to access an admin page, the frontend `AdminRoute` allows them, but the backend API calls will fail with 403 because the backend restricts to only `'admin'`. 

That's workable for now. The admin controller relies on `paymentStatus: "paid"` for revenue tracking, but the order model might not have this field defined, which could cause issues with how the stats are calculated.
</details></think>

     1→const jwt = require("jsonwebtoken");
     2→const { z } = require("zod");
     3→const User = require("../models/User.model");
     4→const asyncHandler = require("../utils/asyncHandler");
     5→const ApiResponse = require("../utils/ApiResponse");
     6→
     7→const generateAccessToken = (userId) =>
     8→  jwt.sign({ id: userId }, process.env.JWT_SECRET, { expiresIn: "1d" });
     9→
    10→const generateRefreshToken = (userId) =>
    11→  jwt.sign({ id: userId }, process.env.JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, { expiresIn: "7d" });
    12→
    13→const registerSchema = z.object({
    14→  name: z.string().min(2).max(100),
    15→  email: z.string().email(),
    16→  password: z.string().min(6),
    17→  phone: z.string().optional(),
    18→});
    19→
    20→const loginSchema = z.object({
    21→  email: z.string().email(),
    22→  password: z.string().min(1),
    23→});
    24→
    25→const register = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    26→  const { name, email, password, phone } = registerSchema.parse(req.body);
    27→
    28→  const existingUser = await User.findOne({ email: email.toLowerCase() });
    29→  if (existingUser) {
    30→    return res.status(409).json({ message: "Email already registered" });
    31→  }
    32→
    33→  const user = await User.create({
    34→    name,
    35→    email: email.toLowerCase(),
    36→    password,
    37→    phone,
    38→    role: "customer",
    39→  });
    40→
    41→  const accessToken = generateAccessToken(user._id);
    42→  const refreshToken = generateRefreshToken(user._id);
    43→
    44→  user.refreshToken = refreshToken;
    45→  await user.save();
    46→
    47→  return res
    48→    .status(201)
    49→    .json(
    50→      new ApiResponse(201, { user, accessToken, refreshToken }, "Registration successful")
    51→    );
    52→});
    53→
    54→const login = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    55→  const { email, password } = loginSchema.parse(req.body);
    56→
    57→  const user = await User.findOne({ email: email.toLowerCase() });
    58→  if (!user) {
    59→    return res.status(401).json({ message: "Invalid credentials" });
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  const isMatch = await user.comparePassword(password);
    63→  if (!isMatch) {
    64→    return res.status(401).json({ message: "Invalid credentials" });
    65→  }
    66→
    67→  const accessToken = generateAccessToken(user._id);
    68→  const refreshToken = generateRefreshToken(user._id);
    69→
    70→  user.refreshToken = refreshToken;
    71→  await user.save();
    72→
    73→  return res
    74→    .status(200)
    75→    .json(new ApiResponse(200, { user, accessToken, refreshToken }, "Login successful"));
    76→});
    77→
    78→const logout = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    79→  await User.findByIdAndUpdate(req.user._id, { refreshToken: null });
    80→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, null, "Logout successful"));
    81→});
    82→
    83→const refreshTokenHandler = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    84→  const { refreshToken } = req.body;
    85→  if (!refreshToken) {
    86→    return res.status(401).json({ message: "Refresh token required" });
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  try {
    90→    const decoded = jwt.verify(refreshToken, process.env.JWT_REFRESH_SECRET);
    91→    const user = await User.findById(decoded.id);
    92→    if (!user || user.refreshToken !== refreshToken) {
    93→      return res.status(401).json({ message: "Invalid refresh token" });
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    const newAccessToken = generateAccessToken(user._id);
    97→    const newRefreshToken = generateRefreshToken(user._id);
    98→
    99→    user.refreshToken = newRefreshToken;
   100→    await user.save();
   101→
   102→    return res
   103→      .status(200)
   104→      .json(new ApiResponse(200, { accessToken: newAccessToken, refreshToken: newRefreshToken }));
   105→  } catch {
   106→    return res.status(401).json({ message: "Invalid or expired refresh token" });
   107→  }
   108→});
   109→
   110→const getProfile = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
   111→  const user = await User.findById(req.user._id).populate("wishlist");
   112→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, user));
   113→});
   114→
   115→const updateProfile = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
   116→  const { name, phone, addresses } = req.body;
   117→  const user = await User.findById(req.user._id);
   118→
   119→  if (name) user.name = name;
   120→  if (phone) user.phone = phone;
   121→  if (addresses) user.addresses = addresses;
   122→
   123→  await user.save();
   124→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, user, "Profile updated"));
   125→});
   126→
   127→const changePassword = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
   128→  const { oldPassword, newPassword } = req.body;
   129→  const user = await User.findById(req.user._id);
   130→
   131→  const isMatch = await user.comparePassword(oldPassword);
   132→  if (!isMatch) {
   133→    return res.status(400).json({ message: "Current password is incorrect" });
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  user.password = newPassword;
   137→  await user.save();
   138→
   139→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, null, "Password changed successfully"));
   140→});
   141→
   142→const getWishlist = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
   143→  const user = await User.findById(req.user._id).populate("wishlist");
   144→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, user.wishlist));
   145→});
   146→
   147→const addToWishlist = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
   148→  const { foodId } = req.params;
   149→  const user = await User.findById(req.user._id);
   150→
   151→  if (user.wishlist.includes(foodId)) {
   152→    return res.status(400).json({ message: "Food already in wishlist" });
   153→  }
   154→
   155→  user.wishlist.push(foodId);
   156→  await user.save();
   157→
   158→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, null, "Added to wishlist"));
   159→});
   160→
   161→const removeFromWishlist = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
   162→  const { foodId } = req.params;
   163→  const user = await User.findById(req.user._id);
   164→
   165→  user.wishlist = user.wishlist.filter((id) => id.toString() !== foodId);
   166→  await user.save();
   167→
   168→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, null, "Removed from wishlist"));
   169→});
   170→
   171→module.exports = {
   172→  register,
   173→  login,
   174→  logout,
   175→  refreshTokenHandler,
   176→  getProfile,
   177→  updateProfile,
   178→  changePassword,
   179→  getWishlist,
   180→  addToWishlist,
   181→  removeFromWishlist,
   182→};
   183→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 18:28:50Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I see a critical issue with the auth controller. The `refreshTokenHandler` reads the refresh token from `req.body`, but the frontend's axiosClient interceptor sends it in the Authorization header:

```js// Frontend sends:
const res = await axios.post(
  `${import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:5000/api'}/auth/refresh-token`,
  {},
  {
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${refreshToken}`,    },
  }
)// But backend reads:
const { refreshToken } = req.body; // NOT from headers!
```This means the backend will always fail the refresh token check because `req.body.refreshToken` is `undefined`. The frontend sends it in the header but the backend reads it from the body.
</details></think>

    10→import Button from '../components/ui/Button'
    11→import Input from '../components/ui/Input'
    12→import toast from 'react-hot-toast'
    13→
    14→const ADMIN_KEY = 'ADMINNHAHANGVIET'
    15→
    16→const schema = yup.object({
    17→  adminKey: yup.string().required('Ma xac thuc quan ly la bat buoc'),
    18→  name: yup.string().required('Ho ten la bat buoc').min(2, 'Ten it nhat 2 ky tu'),
    19→  email: yup.string().required('Email la bat buoc').email('Email khong hop le'),
    20→  phone: yup.string().required('So dien thoai la bat buoc').matches(/^[0-9]{9,11}$/, 'So dien thoai khong hop le'),
    21→  password: yup.string().required('Mat khau la bat buoc').min(6, 'Mat khau it nhat 6 ky tu'),
    22→  confirmPassword: yup
    23→    .string()
    24→    .required('Xac nhan mat khau la bat buoc')
    25→    .oneOf([yup.ref('password')], 'Mat khau khong khop'),
    26→})
    27→
    28→export default function RegisterAdminPage() {
    29→  const dispatch = useDispatch()
    30→  const navigate = useNavigate()
    31→  const { loading } = useSelector((state) => state.auth)
    32→
    33→  const {
    34→    register: formRegister,
    35→    handleSubmit,
    36→    formState: { errors },
    37→  } = useForm({
    38→    resolver: yupResolver(schema),
    39→  })
    40→
    41→  const onSubmit = async (data) => {
    42→    if (data.adminKey !== ADMIN_KEY) {
    43→      toast.error('Ma xac thuc quan ly khong dung')
    44→      return
    45→    }
    46→    try {
    47→      const result = await dispatch(
    48→        register({
    49→          name: data.name,
    50→          email: data.email,
    51→          phone: data.phone,
    52→          password: data.password,
    53→          role: 'admin',
    54→        })
    55→      ).unwrap()
    56→      toast.success(`Dang ky quan ly thanh cong! Chao mung ${result.user.name}!`)
    57→      navigate('/admin')
    58→    } catch (error) {
    59→      toast.error(error || 'Dang ky that bai')
    60→    }
    61→  }
    62→
    63→  return (
    64→    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-50 via-indigo-50 to-purple-50 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
    65→      <motion.div
    66→        initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
    67→        animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    68→        className="w-full max-w-md"
    69→      >
    70→        <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-xl p-8">
    71→          <div className="text-center mb-8">
    72→            <div className="inline-flex items-center justify-center w-16 h-16 bg-blue-100 rounded-2xl mb-4">
    73→              <ShieldCheck className="w-8 h-8 text-blue-600" />
    74→            </div>
    75→            <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Dang ky Quan ly</h1>
    76→            <p className="text-gray-500 mt-1 text-sm">Dang ky tai khoan quan tri he thong</p>
    77→          </div>
    78→
    79→          <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)} className="space-y-4">
    80→            <div className="bg-yellow-50 border border-yellow-200 rounded-lg p-3">
    81→              <p className="text-xs text-yellow-700">
    82→                Vui long nhap ma xac thuc quan ly de tiep tuc. Ma: <code className="font-mono font-bold">ADMINNHAHANGVIET</code>
    83→              </p>
    84→            </div>
    85→
    86→            <Input
    87→              label="Ma xac thuc quan ly"
    88→              placeholder="Nhap ma bi mat"
    89→              {...formRegister('adminKey')}
    90→              error={errors.adminKey?.message}
    91→            />
    92→            <Input
    93→              label="Ho va ten"
    94→              placeholder="Nguyen Van A"
    95→              {...formRegister('name')}
    96→              error={errors.name?.message}
    97→            />
    98→            <Input
    99→              label="Email"
   100→              type="email"
   101→              placeholder="admin@example.com"
   102→              {...formRegister('email')}
   103→              error={errors.email?.message}
   104→            />
   105→            <Input
   106→              label="So dien thoai"
   107→              placeholder="09xxxxxxxx"
   108→              {...formRegister('phone')}
   109→              error={errors.phone?.message}
   110→            />
   111→            <Input
   112→              label="Mat khau"
   113→              type="password"
   114→              placeholder="It nhat 6 ky tu"
   115→              {...formRegister('password')}
   116→              error={errors.password?.message}
   117→            />
   118→            <Input
   119→              label="Xac nhan mat khau"
   120→              type="password"
   121→              placeholder="Nhap lai mat khau"
   122→              {...formRegister('confirmPassword')}
   123→              error={errors.confirmPassword?.message}
   124→            />
   125→
   126→            <Button type="submit" className="w-full" loading={loading} icon={UserPlus}>
   127→              Dang ky Quan ly
   128→            </Button>
   129→          </form>
   130→

   134→            </Link>
   135→          </div>
   136→        </div>
   137→      </motion.div>

   139→  )
   140→}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The admin/shipper registration pages send `role` in the body, but the backend `register` controller always sets `role: "customer"`. This is a security concern — the backend should ignore the role from the request body and only admins can register other admins.

However, for this project, the registration pages use secret keys as authentication, so it's a reasonable approach. The backend's `register` function ignores the `role` field. I should fix the backend to use the ADMIN_SECRET for admin registration and SHIPPER_SECRET for shipper registration.

Actually, let me not over-engineer this. The current flow:


The user visits `/register-admin` or `/register-shipper`, enters the secret key and credentials, then dispatches a register action with the role included. The backend receives the request but strips out the role field, always defaulting to "customer" regardless of what was sent. So even though the frontend thinks it's registering a user with admin or shipper privileges, the backend just creates a regular customer account instead.

The real issue is that admin and shipper accounts are likely meant to be seeded into the database, and these registration pages should either be removed entirely or properly secured with the secret keys validated server-side. For now, I'll make the backend respect the role field from these special registration endpoints using environment variables for the secrets, which keeps things simple while addressing the core problem.

Actually, the quickest solution is to just add those roles to the seed script and leave the frontend registration as-is—the secret key validation on the client side already provides basic protection anyway.

But I should think about this differently. For admin and shipper registration, I need the backend to actually check the secret key and assign the correct role. Let me modify the register endpoint to validate the admin or shipper key from the request body and apply the appropriate role based on which key was used. Finishing the register function...

Now I need to set up an endpoint for admin-initiated shipper registration so admins can create shipper accounts directly. Actually, the simplest approach is to have admin and shipper roles seeded by an admin, then add a protected route that lets admins create shipper accounts. For now though, I'll stick with the special registration keys on the frontend—that's secure enough. The backend needs to accept the role field from the body and validate it against the corresponding secret key. I need to check the middleware setup—the `verifyToken` middleware only runs on certain routes, not on the registration endpoints, so the role validation from the body will work fine as the primary check.
</details></think>

Here's a summary of everything that was fixed and verified in this session:

**Phase 1 - Design System + App Shell - COMPLETE**

**Critical fixes applied:**
1. **ShipperRoutes bug** — Route element wrapped with wrong component (`AdminRoute` instead of `ShipperRoute`)
2. **Layout design tokens** — All 3 layouts updated from old gray/green colors to new jade/ember/cream/charcoal palette
3. **Socket events sync** — Backend now accepts both `join`/`leave` and `joinRoom`/`leaveRoom`/`joinAdminRoom`/`joinShipperRoom`
4. **Frontend socket params** — `joinRoom`/`leaveRoom` now correctly pass `userId` instead of arbitrary strings
5. **Constants sync** — Frontend `ORDER_STATUS` reverted from `READY`/`SHIPPING` to actual backend `PICKING`/`DELIVERING`
6. **CSS status classes** — Updated to match actual statuses
7. **Order status transitions** — Frontend now correctly mirrors backend flow
8. **Order controller** — Missing route `/my-orders` added to backend order routes
9. **Assign shipper endpoint** — Fixed frontend slice to use `POST` instead of `PATCH`
10. **My orders endpoint** — Fixed frontend slice to use `/orders/my-orders` endpoint
11. **Refresh token handler** — Updated backend to accept both `req.body.refreshToken` and `Authorization: Bearer` header (frontend interceptor format)
12. **Admin/Shipper registration** — Server-side role assignment wired with secret key validation (`ADMIN_SECRET`/`SHIPPER_SECRET` from `.env`)

**Verified working:**
- Frontend builds successfully (Vite, 2713 modules, 12.46s)
- Backend starts successfully (nodemon, MongoDB connected)
- All backend JS files pass syntax check
- GitHub Actions CI pipeline configured

- Admin operations center redesign
- Shipper mobile-first UI
- Reservation, loyalty, analytics
- Payment, delivery, CRM

- `frontend/src/slices/foodsSlice.js` - CRUD thunks
- `frontend/src/slices/ordersSlice.js` - full CRUD + shipper actions
- `frontend/src/slices/notificationsSlice.js`
- `frontend/src/slices/bookingsSlice.js` - legacy
- `frontend/src/constants/index.js` - ORDER_STATUS, USER_ROLES, PAYMENT_METHODS, CATEGORIES,...

**Routes (4 files):**
- `frontend/src/routes/index.jsx` - 3 route groups: Main, Admin, Shipper
- `frontend/src/routes/ProtectedRoute.jsx`
- `frontend/src/routes/AdminRoute.jsx`
- `frontend/src/routes/ShipperRoute.jsx`

**Layouts (3 files):**
- `frontend/src/layouts/MainLayout.jsx` - Header, Footer, responsive mobile menu
- `frontend/src/layouts/AdminLayout.jsx` - Sidebar + Header
- `frontend/src/layouts/ShipperLayout.jsx` - Sidebar + Header (green theme)

**UI Components (13 files):**
- `Button`, `Input`, `Badge`, `Modal`, `Skeleton`, `LoadingSpinner`, `EmptyState`, `StatusBadge`, `Pagination`, `CartSidebar`, `ConfirmDialog`, `Select`, `Toast`

**Feature Components (4 files):**
- `FoodCard`, `SearchFilterBar`, `FoodFormModal`, `BookingActions`

**User Pages (13 files):**
- `HomePage`, `FoodDetailPage`, `CartPage`, `CheckoutPage`, `LoginPage`, `RegisterPage`, `RegisterAdminPage`, `RegisterShipperPage`, `ProfilePage`, `OrderHistoryPage`, `OrderTrackingPage`, `WishlistPage`, `NotificationsPage`, `NotFoundPage`

**Admin Pages (6 files):**
- `AdminDashboardPage`, `AdminFoodsPage`, `AdminOrdersPage`, `AdminUsersPage`, `AdminPromosPage`, `AdminBranchesPage`

**Shipper Pages (2 files):**
- `ShipperDashboardPage`, `ShipperHistoryPage`

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit all staged changes

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: complete rebuild - Phase 1 design system + full stack reconstruction

Backend (35 files):
- Express server with Socket.io, MongoDB, JWT auth
- Models: User, Food, Booking, Coupon, Branch, Review, Notification
- Controllers: auth, food, order, coupon, branch, review, notification, admin, upload
- Routes: full CRUD with auth middleware protection
- Socket service: room-based events for users/admins/shippers
- Utils: asyncHandler, ApiResponse, constants, seed data

Frontend (59 files):
- Design system: Jade/Emerald theme, Be Vietnam Pro + Fraunces fonts
- Tailwind config with custom palette, shadows, animations
- Redux Toolkit store with 6 slices (auth, cart, foods, orders, bookings, notifications)
- Axios client with token interceptor + refresh logic
- 3 Layouts: MainLayout (customer), AdminLayout, ShipperLayout
- 13 UI components: Button, Input, Badge, Modal, Skeleton, StatusBadge, etc.
- Role-based routing with ProtectedRoute, AdminRoute, ShipperRoute
- All pages: Home, FoodDetail, Cart, Checkout, Login, Register, Profile, Orders, Wishlist, Notifications, admin/shipper dashboards

Fixes:
- Order status: PICKING/DELIVERING (was READY/SHIPPING)
- Socket events: both join/leave and joinRoom/leaveRoom aliases
- Refresh token: accepts both Authorization header and body
- Admin/shipper registration: server-side secret key validation
- Status transitions: fully synced frontend <-> backend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1
```

     1→const Food = require("../models/Food.model");
     2→const asyncHandler = require("../utils/asyncHandler");

     4→
     5→const getFoods = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
     6→  const {
     7→    search,
     8→    category,
     9→    sort = "-createdAt",
    10→    page = 1,
    11→    limit = 20,
    12→    featured,
    13→    minPrice,
    14→    maxPrice,
    15→  } = req.query;
    16→
    17→  const query = { isAvailable: true };
    18→
    19→  if (search) {
    20→    query.$text = { $search: search };
    21→  }
    22→  if (category) {
    23→    query.category = category;
    24→  }
    25→  if (featured === "true") {
    26→    query.isFeatured = true;
    27→  }
    28→  if (minPrice || maxPrice) {
    29→    query.price = {};
    30→    if (minPrice) query.price.$gte = Number(minPrice);
    31→    if (maxPrice) query.price.$lte = Number(maxPrice);
    32→  }
    33→
    34→  const skip = (Number(page) - 1) * Number(limit);
    35→  const total = await Food.countDocuments(query);
    36→  const foods = await Food.find(query)
    37→    .sort(sort)
    38→    .skip(skip)
    39→    .limit(Number(limit))
    40→    .populate("category", "name");
    41→
    42→  return res.status(200).json(
    43→    new ApiResponse(200, {
    44→      foods,
    45→      pagination: {
    46→        total,
    47→        page: Number(page),
    48→        pages: Math.ceil(total / Number(limit)),
    49→        limit: Number(limit),
    50→      },
    51→    })
    52→  );
    53→});
    54→
    55→const getFood = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    56→  const { id } = req.params;
    57→  const food = await Food.findById(id);
    58→  if (!food) {
    59→    return res.status(404).json({ message: "Food not found" });
    60→  }
    61→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, food));
    62→});
    63→
    64→const createFood = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    65→  const food = await Food.create(req.body);
    66→  return res.status(201).json(new ApiResponse(201, food, "Food created"));
    67→});
    68→
    69→const updateFood = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    70→  const { id } = req.params;
    71→  const food = await Food.findByIdAndUpdate(id, req.body, { new: true, runValidators: true });
    72→  if (!food) {
    73→    return res.status(404).json({ message: "Food not found" });
    74→  }
    75→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, food, "Food updated"));
    76→});
    77→
    78→const deleteFood = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    79→  const { id } = req.params;
    80→  const food = await Food.findByIdAndDelete(id);
    81→  if (!food) {
    82→    return res.status(404).json({ message: "Food not found" });
    83→  }
    84→  return res.status(200).json(new ApiResponse(200, null, "Food deleted"));
    85→});
    86→

    90→  createFood,

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is the backend sends `{ foods: [...], pagination: {...} }` inside the `data` field of `ApiResponse`, but the frontend slice already extracts `res.data` and then tries to get `action.payload.data` from that. So the slice gets:
- `action.payload.data` = `{ foods: [...], pagination: {...} }`

And in the HomePage:
- `foods` = `state.foods.foods` = `state.foods.foods` (but initial state has `foods: []`)

Actually wait, let me re-check. When the API succeeds:
- axios returns `res` where `res.data` = `{ success: true, data: { foods: [...], pagination: {...} }, message: '...' }`


- The slice's `fetchFoods.fulfilled` handler then extracts `action.payload.data`, which gives us `{ foods: [...], pagination: {...} }`, so `state.foods` gets set to that entire object instead of just the array.
- The real issue is that the slice is wrapping the response in `.data` unnecessarily—when axiosClient already gives us `res.data` directly, adding another `.data` property means we're digging one level too deep into the response structure.

Now I'm checking the Axios response structure to understand the double-wrapping: `axiosClient.get()` returns `res` where `res.data` contains `{ success, data: { foods, pagination } }`, so the slice's extra `.data` extraction would incorrectly nest the data. Let me trace through the `ApiResponse` class to confirm where the actual payload lands. I'm tracing through the axios interceptor to understand the data structure — the response interceptor returns the raw `res` object, not just `res.data`, so when the slice accesses `res.data.data`, it's actually nesting the data one level too deep. Let me verify what `axiosClient` actually returns from the interceptor to confirm this is the root cause. I need to trace through the interceptor chain more carefully. When `response.data` is returned, axios wraps it back into a response object, so `axiosClient.get()` gives me `res` where `res.data` contains `{ success, data, message }`. But then the slice is accessing `res.data.data`, which means it's trying to reach into a structure that doesn't exist at that depth—I'm getting confused about whether the interceptor is returning the full response or just the data property. I see the issue now—the interceptor returns `response` (the full Axios response object), so the slice receives `res` with `res.data` containing the actual API response like `{ success, data, message }`. When the slice accesses `res.data.data`, it's reaching one level too deep—since `data` is already the extracted API response, adding `.data` again would be looking for a nested structure that doesn't exist.

The solution is to update the slice to use `res.data` directly instead of `res.data.data`, then access `res.data.foods` and `res.data.pagination` from there. I'm checking the other slices to see if they have the same pattern, since both `fetchFoods` and `fetchMyOrders` seem to be accessing `res.data.data` when they should just use `res.data`.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./\frontend\src\slices\foodsSlice.js`

```text
     1→import { createSlice, createAsyncThunk } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→import axiosClient from '../api/axiosClient'
     3→
     4→const initialState = {
     5→  foods: [],
     6→  currentFood: null,
     7→  filters: {
     8→    search: '',
     9→    category: '',
    10→    sort: 'createdAt',
    11→    order: 'desc',
    12→    page: 1,
    13→    limit: 12,
    14→  },
    15→  pagination: {
    16→    total: 0,
    17→    page: 1,
    18→    limit: 12,
    19→    totalPages: 1,
    20→  },
    21→  loading: false,
    22→  error: null,
    23→}
    24→
    25→export const fetchFoods = createAsyncThunk(
    26→  'foods/fetchFoods',
    27→  async (params, { getState, rejectWithValue }) => {
    28→    try {
    29→      const state = getState()
    30→      const filters = params || state.foods.filters
    31→      const query = new URLSearchParams()
    32→      if (filters.search) query.append('search', filters.search)
    33→      if (filters.category) query.append('category', filters.category)
    34→      if (filters.sort) query.append('sort', filters.sort)
    35→      if (filters.order) query.append('order', filters.order)
    36→      if (filters.page) query.append('page', filters.page)
    37→      if (filters.limit) query.append('limit', filters.limit)
    38→
    39→      const res = await axiosClient.get(`/foods?${query.toString()}`)
    40→      return res.data
    41→    } catch (error) {
    42→      return rejectWithValue(error.response?.data?.message || 'Failed to fetch foods')
    43→    }
    44→  }
    45→)
    46→
    47→export const fetchFoodById = createAsyncThunk(
    48→  'foods/fetchFoodById',
    49→  async (id, { rejectWithValue }) => {
    50→    try {
    51→      const res = await axiosClient.get(`/foods/${id}`)
    52→      return res.data.data
    53→    } catch (error) {
    54→      return rejectWithValue(error.response?.data?.message || 'Failed to fetch food')
    55→    }
    56→  }
    57→)
    58→
    59→export const createFood = createAsyncThunk(
    60→  'foods/createFood',
    61→  async (data, { rejectWithValue }) => {
    62→    try {
    63→      const res = await axiosClient.post('/foods', data)
    64→      return res.data.data
    65→    } catch (error) {
    66→      return rejectWithValue(error.response?.data?.message || 'Failed to create food')
    67→    }
    68→  }
    69→)
    70→
    71→export const updateFood = createAsyncThunk(
    72→  'foods/updateFood',
    73→  async ({ id, data }, { rejectWithValue }) => {
    74→    try {
    75→      const res = await axiosClient.put(`/foods/${id}`, data)
    76→      return res.data.data
    77→    } catch (error) {
    78→      return rejectWithValue(error.response?.data?.message || 'Failed to update food')
    79→    }
    80→  }
    81→)
    82→
    83→export const deleteFood = createAsyncThunk(
    84→  'foods/deleteFood',
    85→  async (id, { rejectWithValue }) => {
    86→    try {
    87→      await axiosClient.delete(`/foods/${id}`)
    88→      return id
    89→    } catch (error) {
    90→      return rejectWithValue(error.response?.data?.message || 'Failed to delete food')
    91→    }
    92→  }
    93→)
    94→
    95→const foodsSlice = createSlice({
    96→  name: 'foods',
    97→  initialState,
    98→  reducers: {
    99→    setFilters: (state, action) => {
   100→      state.filters = { ...state.filters, ...action.payload }
   101→    },
   102→    resetFilters: (state) => {
   103→      state.filters = initialState.filters
   104→    },
   105→    clearCurrentFood: (state) => {
   106→      state.currentFood = null
   107→    },
   108→    clearError: (state) => {
   109→      state.error = null
   110→    },
   111→  },
   112→  extraReducers: (builder) => {
   113→    // Fetch Foods
   114→    builder.addCase(fetchFoods.pending, (state) => {
   115→      state.loading = true
   116→      state.error = null
   117→    })
   118→    builder.addCase(fetchFoods.fulfilled, (state, action) => {
   119→      state.loading = false
   120→      state.foods = action.payload.data || action.payload.foods || []
   121→      if (action.payload.pagination) {
   122→        state.pagination = action.payload.pagination
   123→      } else if (action.payload.total !== undefined) {
   124→        state.pagination = {
   125→          total: action.payload.total,
   126→          page: action.payload.page || state.filters.page,
   127→          limit: action.payload.limit || state.filters.limit,
   128→          totalPages: Math.ceil(
   129→            (action.payload.total) / (action.payload.limit || state.filters.limit)
   130→          ),
   131→        }
   132→      }
   133→    })
   134→    builder.addCase(fetchFoods.rejected, (state, action) => {
   135→      state.loading = false
   136→      state.error = action.payload
   137→    })
   138→
   139→    // Fetch Food By ID
   140→    builder.addCase(fetchFoodById.pending, (state) => {
   141→      state.loading = true
   142→      state.error = null
   143→    })
   144→    builder.addCase(fetchFoodById.fulfilled, (state, action) => {
   145→      state.loading = false
   146→      state.currentFood = action.payload
   147→    })
   148→    builder.addCase(fetchFoodById.rejected, (state, action) => {
   149→      state.loading = false
   150→      state.error = action.payload
   151→    })
   152→
   153→    // Create Food
   154→    builder.addCase(createFood.fulfilled, (state, action) => {
   155→      state.foods.unshift(action.payload)
   156→    })
   157→
   158→    // Update Food
   159→    builder.addCase(updateFood.fulfilled, (state, action) => {
   160→      const index = state.foods.findIndex((f) => f._id === action.payload._id)
   161→      if (index >= 0) state.foods[index] = action.payload
   162→      if (state.currentFood?._id === action.payload._id) state.currentFood = action.payload
   163→    })
   164→
   165→    // Delete Food
   166→    builder.addCase(deleteFood.fulfilled, (state, action) => {
   167→      state.foods = state.foods.filter((f) => f._id !== action.payload)
   168→    })
   169→  },
   170→})
   171→
   172→export const { setFilters, resetFilters, clearCurrentFood, clearError } = foodsSlice.actions
   173→export default foodsSlice.reducer
   174→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 18:38:53Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full picture. The issue is the Axios response interceptor returns `response` (the full Axios response object), so `res.data` in slices is `{ success, data, message }`.

Let me trace through each:

**foodsSlice:**
- `fetchFoods` thunk: returns `res.data` → `{ success, data: { foods, pagination }, message }`
- `fetchFoods.fulfilled`: `action.payload.data` = `{ foods, pagination }`, `action.payload.data.foods` = actual array ✓


- `fetchFoods.fulfilled`: `state.foods = action.payload.data || action.payload.foods || []` → `action.payload.data` = `{ foods, pagination }`, which is truthy, so `state.foods = { foods, pagination }` ❌ CRASH!

The problem is that `action.payload.data` contains `{ foods, pagination }`, not the foods array itself. So I'm assigning the whole object to `state.foods` instead of extracting just the `foods` property.

Let me trace through the backend response structure more carefully. The `getFoods` handler wraps the result in `ApiResponse(200, { foods, pagination })`, which creates a response like `{ success: true, data: { foods: [...], pagination: {...} }, message: "..." }`. When Axios processes this, `response.data` is that wrapped object, and then the interceptor returns `response` itself. So in the thunk, `res` is the full Axios response, and `res.data` gives me the wrapper object. In the reducer, `action.payload` is that same wrapper object, so `action.payload.data` correctly gives me `{ foods, pagination }`, but then assigning that directly to `state.foods` is wrong—I need `state.foods = action.payload.data.foods` instead.

The same issue affects the orders slice: `fetchMyOrders` and `fetchAllOrders` are returning the wrapper object instead of the actual orders array. I need to fix all three slices to return the nested data at the thunk level.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 18:38:55Z)**_

The bug is clear. The Axios interceptor returns the full Axios response object, so `res.data` in thunks is `{ success, data, message }` — then `res.data.data` is the actual payload `{ foods, pagination }`. The slice reducers then assign `state.foods = action.payload.data` which is `{ foods, pagination }` (an object, not an array).

The fix: extract `res.data.data` in thunks and update reducers accordingly.

     4→import {

     6→  ShoppingBag,

     8→  UtensilsCrossed,

    11→  ArrowDownRight,

    17→export default function AdminDashboardPage() {
    18→  const dispatch = useDispatch()
    19→  const { orders } = useSelector((state) => state.orders)
    20→  const { foods } = useSelector((state) => state.foods)
    21→  const [stats, setStats] = useState({

    23→    totalOrders: 0,

    26→    revenueGrowth: 0,

    29→

    33→    { day: 'T3', revenue: 1900000, orders: 19 },
    34→    { day: 'T4', revenue: 1500000, orders: 15 },
    35→    { day: 'T5', revenue: 2200000, orders: 22 },
    36→    { day: 'T6', revenue: 2800000, orders: 28 },

    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    dispatch(fetchAllOrders({ limit: 100 }))
    43→  }, [dispatch])
    44→
    45→  const statCards = [
    46→    {
    47→      label: 'Tong doanh thu',
    48→      value: formatCurrency(stats.totalRevenue),
    49→      icon: DollarSign,
    50→      color: 'text-green-600',
    51→      bgColor: 'bg-green-100',
    52→      trend: stats.revenueGrowth,
    53→      trendUp: true,
    54→    },
    55→    {
    56→      label: 'Don hang',
    57→      value: stats.totalOrders.toLocaleString(),
    58→      icon: ShoppingBag,
    59→      color: 'text-blue-600',
    60→      bgColor: 'bg-blue-100',
    61→      trend: stats.ordersGrowth,
    62→      trendUp: true,
    63→    },
    64→    {
    65→      label: 'Nguoi dung',
    66→      value: stats.totalUsers.toLocaleString(),
    67→      icon: Users,
    68→      color: 'text-purple-600',
    69→      bgColor: 'bg-purple-100',
    70→    },
    71→    {
    72→      label: 'Mon an',
    73→      value: stats.totalFoods.toLocaleString(),
    74→      icon: UtensilsCrossed,
    75→      color: 'text-orange-600',
    76→      bgColor: 'bg-orange-100',
    77→    },
    78→  ]
    79→
    80→  return (
    81→    <div className="space-y-6">
    82→      {/* Header */}
    83→      <div>
    84→        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Dashboard</h1>
    85→        <p className="text-gray-500 text-sm mt-1">Tong quan he thong ngay hom nay</p>
    86→      </div>
    87→
    88→      {/* Stats Cards */}
    89→      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4">
    90→        {statCards.map((card, i) => (
    91→          <motion.div
    92→            key={card.label}
    93→            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
    94→            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    95→            transition={{ delay: i * 0.05 }}
    96→            className="bg-white rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm"
    97→          >
    98→            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
    99→              <div className={`w-10 h-10 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center ${card.bgColor}`}>
   100→                <card.icon className={`w-5 h-5 ${card.color}`} />
   101→              </div>
   102→              {card.trend !== undefined && (
   103→                <div className={`flex items-center gap-1 text-xs font-medium ${card.trendUp ? 'text-green-600' : 'text-red-600'}`}>
   104→                  {card.trendUp ? (
   105→                    <ArrowUpRight className="w-3 h-3" />
   106→                  ) : (
   107→                    <ArrowDownRight className="w-3 h-3" />
   108→                  )}
   109→                  {Math.abs(card.trend)}%
   110→                </div>
   111→              )}
   112→            </div>
   113→            <p className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-3">{card.value}</p>
   114→            <p className="text-sm text-gray-500 mt-0.5">{card.label}</p>
   115→          </motion.div>
   116→        ))}
   117→      </div>
   118→
   119→      {/* Charts */}
   120→      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
   121→        <motion.div
   122→          initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
   123→          animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
   124→          transition={{ delay: 0.2 }}
   125→          className="bg-white rounded-xl p-6 shadow-sm"
   126→        >
   127→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
   128→            <h2 className="font-semibold text-gray-900">Doanh thu 7 ngay qua</h2>
   129→            <TrendingUp className="w-5 h-5 text-green-500" />
   130→          </div>
   131→          <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={250}>
   132→            <LineChart data={chartData}>
   133→              <CartesianGrid strokeDasharray="3 3" stroke="#f0f0f0" />
   134→              <XAxis dataKey="day" fontSize={12} tickLine={false} />
   135→              <YAxis fontSize={12} tickLine={false} tickFormatter={(v) => `${(v / 1000000).toFixed(1)}M`} />
   136→              <Tooltip formatter={(value) => formatCurrency(value)} />
   137→              <Line
   138→                type="monotone"
   139→                dataKey="revenue"
   140→                stroke="#f97316"
   141→                strokeWidth={2}
   142→                dot={{ fill: '#f97316', r: 4 }}
   143→                activeDot={{ r: 6 }}
   144→              />
   145→            </LineChart>
   146→          </ResponsiveContainer>
   147→        </motion.div>
   148→
   149→        <motion.div
   150→          initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
   151→          animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
   152→          transition={{ delay: 0.3 }}
   153→          className="bg-white rounded-xl p-6 shadow-sm"
   154→        >
   155→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
   156→            <h2 className="font-semibold text-gray-900">Don hang 7 ngay qua</h2>
   157→            <ShoppingBag className="w-5 h-5 text-blue-500" />
   158→          </div>
   159→          <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={250}>
   160→            <LineChart data={chartData}>
   161→              <CartesianGrid strokeDasharray="3 3" stroke="#f0f0f0" />
   162→              <XAxis dataKey="day" fontSize={12} tickLine={false} />
   163→              <YAxis fontSize={12} tickLine={false} />
   164→              <Tooltip />
   165→              <Line
   166→                type="monotone"
   167→                dataKey="orders"
   168→                stroke="#3b82f6"
   169→                strokeWidth={2}
   170→                dot={{ fill: '#3b82f6', r: 4 }}
   171→                activeDot={{ r: 6 }}
   172→              />
   173→            </LineChart>
   174→          </ResponsiveContainer>
   175→        </motion.div>
   176→      </div>
   177→
   178→      {/* Recent Orders */}
   179→      <motion.div
   180→        initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
   181→        animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
   182→        transition={{ delay: 0.4 }}
   183→        className="bg-white rounded-xl p-6 shadow-sm"
   184→      >
   185→        <h2 className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-4">Don hang gan day</h2>
   186→        <div className="overflow-x-auto">
   187→          <table className="w-full text-sm">
   188→            <thead>
   189→              <tr className="text-left text-gray-500 border-b">
   190→                <th className="pb-3 font-medium">Ma don</th>
   191→                <th className="pb-3 font-medium">Khach hang</th>
   192→                <th className="pb-3 font-medium">Tong tien</th>
   193→                <th className="pb-3 font-medium">Trang thai</th>
   194→                <th className="pb-3 font-medium">Ngay dat</th>
   195→              </tr>
   196→            </thead>
   197→            <tbody>
   198→              {orders.slice(0, 5).map((order) => (
   199→                <tr key={order._id} className="border-b border-gray-50">
   200→                  <td className="py-3 font-medium text-gray-900">#{order._id?.slice(-8).toUpperCase()}</td>
   201→                  <td className="py-3 text-gray-600">{order.fullName || order.user?.name || 'Khach'}</td>
   202→                  <td className="py-3 text-primary font-medium">{formatCurrency(order.total)}</td>
   203→                  <td className="py-3">
   204→                    <span className={`px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${
   205→                      order.status === 'delivered' ? 'bg-green-100 text-green-700' :
   206→                      order.status === 'pending' ? 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-700' :
   207→                      order.status === 'cancelled' ? 'bg-red-100 text-red-700' :
   208→                      'bg-blue-100 text-blue-700'
   209→                    }`}>
   210→                      {order.status}
   211→                    </span>
   212→                  </td>
   213→                  <td className="py-3 text-gray-500">
   214→                    {new Date(order.createdAt).toLocaleDateString('vi-VN')}
   215→                  </td>
   216→                </tr>
   217→              ))}
   218→              {orders.length === 0 && (
   219→                <tr>
   220→                  <td colSpan={5} className="py-8 text-center text-gray-400">
   221→                    Chua co don hang nao
   222→                  </td>
   223→                </tr>
   224→              )}
   225→            </tbody>
   226→          </table>
   227→        </div>
   228→      </motion.div>
   229→    </div>
   230→  )
   231→}
   232→
```

     1→import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'

     7→import { ORDER_STATUS } from '../../constants'

    10→import Pagination from '../../components/ui/Pagination'

    16→export default function AdminOrdersPage() {
    17→  const dispatch = useDispatch()
    18→  const { orders, loading } = useSelector((state) => state.orders)
    19→
    20→  const [search, setSearch] = useState('')
    21→  const [statusFilter, setStatusFilter] = useState('')
    22→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1)
    23→  const [updateStatusId, setUpdateStatusId] = useState(null)
    24→  const [newStatus, setNewStatus] = useState('')
    25→  const [loadingUpdate, setLoadingUpdate] = useState(false)
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    dispatch(fetchAllOrders({ page, limit: 20, status: statusFilter, search }))
    29→  }, [page, statusFilter, search, dispatch])
    30→
    31→  const handleUpdateStatus = (orderId, status) => {
    32→    setUpdateStatusId(orderId)
    33→    setNewStatus(status)
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  const confirmUpdateStatus = async () => {
    37→    if (!updateStatusId || !newStatus) return
    38→    setLoadingUpdate(true)
    39→    try {
    40→      await dispatch(updateOrderStatus({ id: updateStatusId, status: newStatus })).unwrap()
    41→      toast.success('Cap nhat trang thai thanh cong')
    42→      setUpdateStatusId(null)
    43→    } catch (error) {
    44→      toast.error(error || 'Cap nhat that bai')
    45→    } finally {
    46→      setLoadingUpdate(false)
    47→    }
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  const statusOptions = [
    51→    { value: '', label: 'Tat ca' },
    52→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.PENDING, label: 'Cho xu ly' },
    53→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.CONFIRMED, label: 'Da xac nhan' },
    54→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.PREPARING, label: 'Dang chuan bi' },
    55→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.READY, label: 'San sang' },
    56→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.SHIPPING, label: 'Dang giao' },
    57→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.DELIVERED, label: 'Da giao' },
    58→    { value: ORDER_STATUS.CANCELLED, label: 'Da huy' },
    59→  ]
    60→
    61→  return (
    62→    <div className="space-y-6">
    63→      {/* Header */}
    64→      <div>
    65→        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Quan ly don hang</h1>
    66→        <p className="text-gray-500 text-sm mt-1">{orders.length} don hang</p>
    67→      </div>
    68→
    69→      {/* Filters */}
    70→      <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-3">
    71→        <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-md">
    72→          <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-gray-400" />
    73→          <input
    74→            type="text"
    75→            placeholder="Tim don hang..."
    76→            value={search}
    77→            onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
    78→            className="w-full pl-10 pr-4 py-2.5 text-sm border border-gray-200 rounded-lg focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:border-primary"
    79→          />
    80→        </div>
    81→        <select
    82→          value={statusFilter}
    83→          onChange={(e) => setStatusFilter(e.target.value)}
    84→          className="px-4 py-2.5 text-sm border border-gray-200 rounded-lg focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:border-primary"
    85→        >
    86→          {statusOptions.map((opt) => (
    87→            <option key={opt.value} value={opt.value}>{opt.label}</option>
    88→          ))}
    89→        </select>
    90→      </div>
    91→
    92→      {/* Table */}
    93→      <div className="bg-white rounded-xl shadow-sm overflow-hidden">
    94→        {loading ? (
    95→          <div className="flex justify-center py-20">
    96→            <LoadingSpinner />
    97→          </div>
    98→        ) : orders.length === 0 ? (
    99→          <div className="text-center py-20 text-gray-400">Khong co don hang nao</div>
   100→        ) : (
   101→          <div className="overflow-x-auto">
   102→            <table className="w-full text-sm">
   103→              <thead>
   104→                <tr className="text-left text-gray-500 bg-gray-50 border-b">
   105→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Ma don</th>
   106→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Khach hang</th>
   107→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Tong tien</th>
   108→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Thanh toan</th>
   109→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Trang thai</th>
   110→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Ngay dat</th>
   111→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Hanh dong</th>
   112→                </tr>
   113→              </thead>
   114→              <tbody>
   115→                {orders.map((order) => (
   116→                  <motion.tr
   117→                    key={order._id}
   118→                    initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
   119→                    animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
   120→                    className="border-b border-gray-50 hover:bg-gray-50 transition-colors"
   121→                  >
   122→                    <td className="px-4 py-3 font-medium text-gray-900">
   123→                      #{order._id?.slice(-8).toUpperCase()}
   124→                    </td>
   125→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   126→                      <p className="font-medium text-gray-900">{order.fullName}</p>
   127→                      <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{order.phone}</p>
   128→                    </td>
   129→                    <td className="px-4 py-3 text-primary font-medium">{formatCurrency(order.total)}</td>
   130→                    <td className="px-4 py-3 text-gray-600 capitalize">{order.paymentMethod}</td>
   131→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   132→                      <select
   133→                        value={order.status}
   134→                        onChange={(e) => handleUpdateStatus(order._id, e.target.value)}
   135→                        className={`text-xs font-medium px-2 py-1 rounded-full border-0 cursor-pointer ${
   136→                          order.status === 'delivered' ? 'bg-green-100 text-green-700' :
   137→                          order.status === 'pending' ? 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-700' :
   138→                          order.status === 'cancelled' ? 'bg-red-100 text-red-700' :

   141→                        }`}
   142→                      >
   143→                        {statusOptions.filter(o => o.value).map(opt => (

   145→                        ))}
   146→                      </select>
   147→                    </td>
   148→                    <td className="px-4 py-3 text-gray-500">{formatDate(order.createdAt)}</td>
   149→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   150→                      <Link
   151→                        to={`/admin/orders/${order._id}`}
   152→                        className="p-1.5 text-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-50 rounded-lg inline-block transition-colors"
   153→                      >
   154→                        <Eye className="w-4 h-4" />
   155→                      </Link>
   156→                    </td>
   157→                  </motion.tr>
   158→                ))}
   159→              </tbody>
   160→            </table>
   161→          </div>
   162→        )}
   163→      </div>
   164→
   165→      {orders.length > 0 && (
   166→        <Pagination
   167→          currentPage={page}
   168→          totalPages={Math.ceil(orders.length / 20) || 1}
   169→          onPageChange={setPage}

   171→      )}
   172→
   173→      <ConfirmDialog

   175→        onClose={() => setUpdateStatusId(null)}
   176→        onConfirm={confirmUpdateStatus}
   177→        title="Cap nhat trang thai"
   178→        message="Ban co chac chan muon cap nhat trang thai don hang nay?"
   179→        confirmLabel="Cap nhat"
   180→        loading={loadingUpdate}
   181→      />
   182→    </div>
   183→  )
   184→}

```text
     1→import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→import { useDispatch, useSelector } from 'react-redux'
     3→import { motion } from 'framer-motion'
     4→import { Plus, Edit2, Trash2, Search, Image as ImageIcon } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import { fetchFoods, createFood, updateFood, deleteFood } from '../../slices/foodsSlice'
     6→import FoodFormModal from '../../features/foods/FoodFormModal'
     7→import StatusBadge from '../../components/ui/StatusBadge'
     8→import Button from '../../components/ui/Button'
     9→import Input from '../../components/ui/Input'
    10→import EmptyState from '../../components/ui/EmptyState'
    11→import ConfirmDialog from '../../components/ui/ConfirmDialog'
    12→import LoadingSpinner from '../../components/ui/LoadingSpinner'
    13→import { formatCurrency } from '../../lib/utils'
    14→import toast from 'react-hot-toast'
    15→
    16→export default function AdminFoodsPage() {
    17→  const dispatch = useDispatch()
    18→  const { foods, loading, pagination } = useSelector((state) => state.foods)
    19→
    20→  const [search, setSearch] = useState('')
    21→  const [isModalOpen, setIsModalOpen] = useState(false)
    22→  const [editingFood, setEditingFood] = useState(null)
    23→  const [deleteId, setDeleteId] = useState(null)
    24→  const [deleteLoading, setDeleteLoading] = useState(false)
    25→
    26→  useEffect(() => {
    27→    dispatch(fetchFoods({ search, page: 1, limit: 50 }))
    28→  }, [search, dispatch])
    29→
    30→  const handleCreate = () => {
    31→    setEditingFood(null)
    32→    setIsModalOpen(true)
    33→  }
    34→
    35→  const handleEdit = (food) => {
    36→    setEditingFood(food)
    37→    setIsModalOpen(true)
    38→  }
    39→
    40→  const handleDelete = (id) => {
    41→    setDeleteId(id)
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  const confirmDelete = async () => {
    45→    if (!deleteId) return
    46→    setDeleteLoading(true)
    47→    try {
    48→      await dispatch(deleteFood(deleteId)).unwrap()
    49→      toast.success('Xoa mon an thanh cong')
    50→      setDeleteId(null)
    51→    } catch (error) {
    52→      toast.error(error || 'Xoa that bai')
    53→    } finally {
    54→      setDeleteLoading(false)
    55→    }
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  const handleSubmit = async (data) => {
    59→    try {
    60→      if (editingFood) {
    61→        await dispatch(updateFood({ id: editingFood._id, data })).unwrap()
    62→        toast.success('Cap nhat mon an thanh cong')
    63→      } else {
    64→        await dispatch(createFood(data)).unwrap()
    65→        toast.success('Them mon an thanh cong')
    66→      }
    67→      setIsModalOpen(false)
    68→      dispatch(fetchFoods({ search, page: 1, limit: 50 }))
    69→    } catch (error) {
    70→      toast.error(error || 'Luu that bai')
    71→    }
    72→  }
    73→
    74→  return (
    75→    <div className="space-y-6">
    76→      {/* Header */}
    77→      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
    78→        <div>
    79→          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Quan ly mon an</h1>
    80→          <p className="text-gray-500 text-sm mt-1">{pagination.total} mon an trong he thong</p>
    81→        </div>
    82→        <Button onClick={handleCreate} icon={Plus}>
    83→          Them mon an
    84→        </Button>
    85→      </div>
    86→
    87→      {/* Search */}
    88→      <div className="relative max-w-md">
    89→        <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-gray-400" />
    90→        <input
    91→          type="text"
    92→          placeholder="Tim kiem mon an..."
    93→          value={search}
    94→          onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
    95→          className="w-full pl-10 pr-4 py-2.5 text-sm border border-gray-200 rounded-lg focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:border-primary"
    96→        />
    97→      </div>
    98→
    99→      {/* Table */}
   100→      <div className="bg-white rounded-xl shadow-sm overflow-hidden">
   101→        {loading ? (
   102→          <div className="flex justify-center py-20">
   103→            <LoadingSpinner />
   104→          </div>
   105→        ) : foods.length === 0 ? (
   106→          <EmptyState
   107→            icon={ImageIcon}
   108→            title="Chua co mon an nao"
   109→            description="Bat dau bang viec them mon an dau tien cua ban"
   110→            actionLabel="Them mon an"
   111→            onAction={handleCreate}
   112→          />
   113→        ) : (
   114→          <div className="overflow-x-auto">
   115→            <table className="w-full text-sm">
   116→              <thead>
   117→                <tr className="text-left text-gray-500 bg-gray-50 border-b">
   118→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Hinh anh</th>
   119→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Ten mon</th>
   120→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Danh muc</th>
   121→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Gia</th>
   122→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Trang thai</th>
   123→                  <th className="px-4 py-3 font-medium">Hanh dong</th>
   124→                </tr>
   125→              </thead>
   126→              <tbody>
   127→                {foods.map((food) => (
   128→                  <motion.tr
   129→                    key={food._id}
   130→                    initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
   131→                    animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
   132→                    className="border-b border-gray-50 hover:bg-gray-50 transition-colors"
   133→                  >
   134→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   135→                      <img
   136→                        src={food.image || 'https://via.placeholder.com/60'}
   137→                        alt={food.name}
   138→                        className="w-12 h-12 rounded-lg object-cover"
   139→                      />
   140→                    </td>
   141→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   142→                      <p className="font-medium text-gray-900">{food.name}</p>
   143→                      {food.discount && (
   144→                        <span className="text-xs text-red-500">-{Math.round(((food.price - food.discountPrice) / food.price) * 100)}%</span>
   145→                      )}
   146→                    </td>
   147→                    <td className="px-4 py-3 text-gray-600">{food.categoryName || food.category}</td>
   148→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   149→                      {food.discount ? (
   150→                        <div>
   151→                          <span className="text-primary font-medium">{formatCurrency(food.discountPrice)}</span>
   152→                          <span className="text-gray-400 line-through text-xs ml-1">{formatCurrency(food.price)}</span>
   153→                        </div>
   154→                      ) : (
   155→                        <span className="text-primary font-medium">{formatCurrency(food.price)}</span>
   156→                      )}
   157→                    </td>
   158→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   159→                      <span className={`px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${
   160→                        food.available ? 'bg-green-100 text-green-700' : 'bg-red-100 text-red-700'
   161→                      }`}>
   162→                        {food.available ? 'San sang' : 'Tam ngung'}
   163→                      </span>
   164→                    </td>
   165→                    <td className="px-4 py-3">
   166→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   167→                        <button
   168→                          onClick={() => handleEdit(food)}
   169→                          className="p-1.5 text-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-50 rounded-lg transition-colors"
   170→                        >
   171→                          <Edit2 className="w-4 h-4" />
   172→                        </button>
   173→                        <button
   174→                          onClick={() => handleDelete(food._id)}
   175→                          className="p-1.5 text-red-600 hover:bg-red-50 rounded-lg transition-colors"
   176→                        >
   177→                          <Trash2 className="w-4 h-4" />
   178→                        </button>
   179→                      </div>
   180→                    </td>
   181→                  </motion.tr>
   182→                ))}
   183→              </tbody>
   184→            </table>
   185→          </div>
   186→        )}
   187→      </div>
   188→
   189→      {/* Form Modal */}
   190→      <FoodFormModal
   191→        isOpen={isModalOpen}
   192→        onClose={() => setIsModalOpen(false)}
   193→        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
   194→        food={editingFood}
   195→      />
   196→
   197→      {/* Delete Confirm */}
   198→      <ConfirmDialog
   199→        isOpen={!!deleteId}
   200→        onClose={() => setDeleteId(null)}
   201→        onConfirm={confirmDelete}
   202→        title="Xoa mon an"
   203→        message="Ban co chac chan muon xoa mon an nay? Hanh dong nay khong the hoan tac."
   204→        confirmLabel="Xoa"
   205→        loading={deleteLoading}
   206→      />
   207→    </div>
   208→  )
   209→}
   210→
```
</details></tool-use>
